name: Dr Jean-François Smekens
school: Department of Astronomy and Planetary Science
bio: Jean-François Smekens is an Assistant Research Professor in the Department of Astronomy and Planetary Science at Northern Arizona University and is affiliated with the Radiant Center for Remote Sensing. His research focuses on the development and application of  remote sensing technologies to study natural hazards and environmental processes. Dr. Smekens works on projects involving spectroscopic and imaging solutions from both ground-based systems and unmanned aerial vehicles, with applications such as monitoring wildfires, volcanic activity, and atmospheric emissions. He contributes to NASA-funded research efforts, including projects aimed at improving real-time hazard assessment and developing new tools for measuring thermal flux, gas output, and particulate emissions. His work integrates remote sensing, data analysis, and environmental modeling to better understand dynamic Earth and planetary systems, bridging planetary science with applied Earth observation.
